_meta.description: NCAA Men's Basketball — table refs + glossary + example queries.

exampleQueries.natural_language_question: Which teams won the NCAA championship in each season?
exampleQueries.sql: SELECT season, win_market, win_name, win_pts, lose_market, lose_pts FROM `bigquery-public-data.ncaa_basketball.mbb_historical_tournament_games` WHERE round = 'Championship' ORDER BY season DESC

exampleQueries.natural_language_question: What are the top 10 highest-scoring NCAA tournament games?
exampleQueries.sql: SELECT season, h_market, a_market, h_points, a_points, (h_points + a_points) AS total_points FROM `bigquery-public-data.ncaa_basketball.mbb_games_sr` WHERE tournament = 'NCAA' ORDER BY total_points DESC LIMIT 10

exampleQueries.natural_language_question: Which team had the most wins in the 2017 season?
exampleQueries.sql: SELECT g.team_id, t.market, COUNT(*) AS wins FROM `bigquery-public-data.ncaa_basketball.mbb_teams_games_sr` g JOIN `bigquery-public-data.ncaa_basketball.mbb_teams` t ON g.team_id = t.id WHERE g.season = 2017 AND g.win = TRUE GROUP BY g.team_id, t.market ORDER BY wins DESC LIMIT 10

exampleQueries.natural_language_question: How has three-point shooting changed over the years?
exampleQueries.sql: SELECT season, AVG(three_points_pct) AS avg_three_pct, AVG(three_points_att) AS avg_three_att FROM `bigquery-public-data.ncaa_basketball.mbb_teams_games_sr` GROUP BY season ORDER BY season

exampleQueries.natural_language_question: Which team has the best all-time win-loss record?
exampleQueries.sql: SELECT h.team_id, t.market, SUM(h.wins) AS total_wins, SUM(h.losses) AS total_losses, ROUND(SUM(h.wins) / (SUM(h.wins) + SUM(h.losses)), 3) AS win_pct FROM `bigquery-public-data.ncaa_basketball.mbb_historical_teams_seasons` h JOIN `bigquery-public-data.ncaa_basketball.mbb_teams` t ON h.team_id = t.id GROUP BY h.team_id, t.market HAVING (SUM(h.wins) + SUM(h.losses)) > 500 ORDER BY win_pct DESC LIMIT 10

glossary_terms.description: The school or city name associated with a team (e.g. 'Duke', 'North Carolina').
Athletic conference a team belongs to (ACC, Big Ten, SEC, etc.). Stored in mbb_teams.conf_name.
Tournament seeding (1-16) assigned to teams in the NCAA March Madness bracket.
The NCAA Division I men's basketball tournament. Filter on tournament = 'NCAA' in mbb_games_sr or use mbb_historical_tournament_games.
The last four teams remaining in the NCAA tournament. tournament_type = 'Final Four'.
Summary statistics for a game — points, rebounds, assists, etc. Available in mbb_teams_games_sr (team level) and mbb_players_games_sr (player level).
Tables with _sr suffix contain detailed data from Sportradar, generally covering 2013-present.
Tables with _historical_ prefix contain data going back further: seasons from 1894-95, games from 1996-97.
